channel.title: Tech Engineering Blog
channel.description: Latest posts from the engineering team

item.title: Migrating to Web Streams
item.dc:creator: Alice
item.description: Learn how we migrated from Node.js streams to the Web Streams API for universal compatibility.
item.content:encoded:
Migrating to Web Streams

The TransformStream API provides a universal streaming primitive that works in browsers, Node.js 18+, Deno, and Bun.

const stream = new TransformStream({
  transform(chunk, controller) {
    controller.enqueue(chunk.toUpperCase());
  }
});

Benefits

  • Universal runtime support
  • No polyfills needed on modern platforms
  • Built-in backpressure handling

"Web Streams are the future of I/O in JavaScript." — TC39 Committee

Migration Checklist

  1. Replace require('stream') with TransformStream
  2. Update transform logic from _transform to transform
  3. Add flush handler for buffered data

item.title: XML Parsing at Scale: Lessons Learned
item.dc:creator: Bob & Carol
item.description: We process millions of XML documents daily. Here's what we've learned about incremental parsing & memory efficiency.
item.content:encoded:
XML Parsing at Scale

Processing large XML documents requires careful attention to memory usage. Streaming parsers that emit nodes incrementally can handle documents of arbitrary size with constant memory overhead.

ParserMemory (MB)Time (ms)
DOM5122400
SAX12890
Streaming8650

Special characters like <, >, &, and "quotes" must be handled correctly in both attributes and text content.

item.title: Deep Dive: TypeScript Generics & Conditional Types
item.dc:creator: Diana
item.description: An advanced guide to TypeScript's type system
item.content:encoded:
Deep Dive: TypeScript Generics

TypeScript's type system is Turing complete, allowing for remarkably expressive type-level programming.

type DeepPartial<T> = {
  [P in keyof T]?: T[P] extends object ? DeepPartial<T[P]> : T[P];
};
